official_title: Courts Rules of Court Order No. 1 of 2015 in respect of the Jurisdiction of the SCT MAY 25, 2015 COURT ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS of Order No. 1DIFC Courts (/glossary/DIFC-courts/) Rules (/glossary/rules/) Court (/glossary/court/) of 2015 In Respect of the of the Small Claims Jurisdiction (/glossary/jurisdiction/) of the DIFC Courts Tribunal (/glossary/tribunal/) I, Michael Hwang, of the DIFC Courts, make the following Chief Justice (/glossary/chief-justice/) Order: In exercise of the powers conferred on me by Article 8(3)(a) of Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004, as amended; and after having reviewed: Dubai Law No. 9 of 2004 in respect of the , as amended;DIFC (/glossary/DIFC/) Dubai Law No. 12 of 2004 in respect of the Judicial Authority at the DIFC, as amended; DIFC Order No. 1 of 2014 in respect of the Rules of the DIFC Courts 1. This Order may be cited as the Rules of Court 2015 Order No. 1 of 2015 2. The DIFC Courts Small Claims Tribunal (SCT) , as set out in the jurisdiction (/glossary/jurisdiction/) Rules of the DIFC Courts (RDC) Part 53.2 shall be amended to read as follows: “The SCT will hear and determine claims within the jurisdiction of the DIFC Courts: (1) where the amount of the claim or the value of the subject matter of the claim does not exceed AED 500,000 [1]or; (2) where the claim relates to the employment or former employment of a party; and all parties elect in writing that it be heard by the SCT (there is no value limit for the SCT’s elective jurisdiction in the context of employment claims); or
